title: RRB Group D 2026
meta_description: RRB Group D 2026 — Track Maintainer, Helper, Porter, and Level 1 posts. 10th pass eligibility, salary ₹18-25K/month, CBT + physical test.

hero.one_liner: Level 1 posts in Indian Railways — Track Maintainer, Helper, Porter, Gateman for 10th pass candidates with ITI preferred

what_is_it: RRB Group D 2026 is one of India's largest recruitment drives with 22,195 vacancies announced for Level 1 (entry-level) posts in Indian Railways. Posts include: Track Maintainer Grade-IV (largest category), Helper in various railway workshops (electrical, mechanical, signal), Porter (station operations), Pointsman (railway traffic control), Gateman (security), and other operational roles. The exam is open to 10th pass candidates (ITI is preferred but not mandatory for most positions).

The recruitment notification was released with application deadline extended to March 9, 2026. Selection process: CBT (Computer Based Test at 10th class level), Physical Efficiency Test (PET — weight lifting and running), Document Verification, and Medical examination. The CBT difficulty is low (10th class standard), making it highly accessible. The PET is the major hurdle — many candidates fail here despite clearing written exam.

Vacancies are distributed across 16 railway zones: Northern Railway (3,537 vacancies), Western Railway (3,148), Central Railway (1,979), and others. Candidates can apply to specific zones and get posting within that zone's jurisdiction. Despite 22,195 vacancies (highest among any exam), competition is intense — 1-2 crore candidates apply, making effective competition ~1 lakh per 200 vacancies (manageable with 3-4 months focused prep).

Starting salary at Level 1 is ₹18,000-21,000 basic + allowances = ₹22,500-25,380 in-hand. While lower than banking exams, railway jobs offer unmatched benefits: free rail travel (worth ₹50,000+/year), free medical (worth ₹30,000+/year), government quarters or HRA, pension, job security, and promotion to Group C (Clerk, Technician) through departmental exams.

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: 10th pass from recognized board. ITI in relevant trade preferred.

key_details.label: PET — Male
key_details.value: Lift 35 kg + carry 100m in 2 min. Run 1000m in 4 min 15 sec.

key_details.label: PET — Female
key_details.value: Lift 20 kg + carry 100m in 2 min. Run 1000m in 5 min 40 sec.

key_details.label: Vision Requirement
key_details.value: Distant vision 6/9 (without glasses). Adequate color vision. Good hearing.

key_details.label: Selection
key_details.value: CBT → PET → Document Verification → Medical Exam

exam_pattern.description: 100 MCQs at 10th class difficulty level. No sectional timing. Bilingual (English + Hindi). Qualifying round; marks don't determine final ranking. PET performance determines final merit.
exam_pattern.negative_marking: 1/3rd of marks deducted per wrong answer

major_posts.department: Indian Railways — Mechanical, Electrical, Signal workshops

extra_sections.heading: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) — Detailed Preparation
content: While CBT is easy (10th class level), PET eliminates 30-40% of candidates. Physical fitness training must start from Day 1 of your exam preparation.
The test includes: (1) Weight carrying (35 kg for males, 20 kg for females) for 100m in 2 minutes, (2) Running 1000m (1 km) in 4 min 15 sec (males) or 5 min 40 sec (females).
The 35 kg weight is placed on your shoulder/back. Key: It's about TECHNIQUE, not pure strength.

Practice: (1) Start with 20 kg sandbag/dumbbell, (2) Walk (don't run) at fast pace, maintaining proper posture, (3) Gradually increase weight and speed, (4) By month 2, carry 35 kg for 100m and time yourself. Average time: 90-110 seconds (well within 2-min limit if you practice properly).

Start running 3 months before PET date: (1) Week 1-4: Run 2 km daily in 10-11 minutes (building endurance), (2) Week 5-8: Run 2 km in 9 minutes, (3) Week 9-12: Run 1 km in 4 min 15 sec (speed work). Use a running app to track pace.
Practice on flat ground, not hills. Run on alternate days (rest important for recovery).
Month 1-2: Build base fitness (running 2 km, weight carrying with light load). Month 2-3: Increase intensity (run 1 km faster, carry heavier weights).
Month 3-4: Taper and test (simulate PET conditions). Final 2 weeks: Light training, focus on recovery and confidence building.
Do not attempt PET without 3 months of consistent training — injury risk is high.

extra_sections.heading: RRB Group D vs Group C/NTPC — Career Comparison
rows: Station Master, Goods Guard, Commercial Apprentice
Group D → Group C (after 5-7 years + exam)

important_dates.label: CBT Exam
important_dates.value: As per RRB schedule (typically 1-2 months after notification close)

preparation_tips: Focus 60% effort on General Science (25 Qs, 25%) — it's high-scoring. Study NCERT Class 6-10 Science thoroughly: physics (motion, force, work, energy, light, electricity), chemistry (acids-bases, metals, carbon compounds), biology (human body systems, diseases, nutrition, ecosystems). Prioritize topics that repeat: force & motion, acids-bases, human anatomy.

General Awareness carries 20 marks — study: Indian geography, national projects (highways, bullet train), recent government schemes, sports news, awards, and railway-specific knowledge (zones, headquarters, recent initiatives like Vande Bharat). Read Dainik Jagran or The Hindu daily for 15 minutes.

Mathematics is 10th class level — cover: number system, BODMAS, percentage, ratio-proportion, profit-loss, simple-compound interest, average, time-work, time-distance, basic geometry, mensuration, and data interpretation. Practice 30 minutes daily using Adda247 or Testbook quizzes.
Start physical training 3 months before PET date — this is non-negotiable. Run daily and practice weight carrying with a sandbag. The exam cuts ~30-40% of candidates here; don't neglect this.

faqs.question: Is Group D salary really only ₹18K? Isn't it too low?
faqs.answer: Starting salary is ₹18,000-21,000 basic, but add allowances (DA, HRA, night duty allowance) = ₹22,500-25,380 in-hand. For 10th pass in Tier-2/3 India, this is competitive. Plus, railway perks (free travel worth ₹50K/year, medical, quarters) make total compensation ~₹30K/month equivalent. After 5-7 years, promotions to Group C increase salary to ₹35,000+/month.

faqs.question: I'm weak in science. Can I still clear Group D?
faqs.answer: Yes. Science is only 25 marks (25% of 100). If you score 15/25 in Science, 20/25 in Math, 25/30 in Reasoning, and 18/20 in GA = 78 total. With ~10 lakh candidates taking the exam and ~5 lakh securing 75+, clearing CBT is achievable without strength in Science. However, science knowledge helps; invest 2 months to build basics.

faqs.question: What happens if I fail PET after clearing CBT?
faqs.answer: You are eliminated. PET performance isn't scored; it's pass/fail. Either you complete weight carry in 2 minutes and run 1000m in time, or you don't qualify further. Document Verification and Medical happen only after PET success. Physical training is critical — don't underestimate.

faqs.question: Can females do Group D Track Maintainer work?
faqs.answer: Yes. RRB encourages female applicants. PET standards for females are relaxed (20 kg instead of 35 kg, 5:40 instead of 4:15). However, Track Maintainer work is outdoor, physically demanding, with shift duties (night shifts included). Females CAN do it, but ensure you're prepared for field work and non-standard timings.

faqs.question: Zones — how to choose for posting?
faqs.answer: During application, select your preferred RRB zone(s). Your posting will be within that zone's jurisdiction (e.g., Northern Railway covers Delhi, Punjab, Jammu-Kashmir regions). You can apply to your home zone or any other; choice is yours. After selection, transfers between zones are possible after 5+ years of service.

syllabus.subject: Mathematics
syllabus.topics: Number system, BODMAS, decimals, fractions, LCM/HCF, percentage, ratio, profit & loss, SI/CI, time & work, time & distance, mensuration, algebra, geometry, basic statistics

syllabus.subject: General Intelligence & Reasoning
syllabus.topics: Analogies, number/alphabet series, coding-decoding, mathematical operations, relationships, syllogism, Venn diagram, conclusion, statement-assumption, decision making

syllabus.subject: General Science
syllabus.topics: Physics (10th level), chemistry (10th level), biology & life sciences (10th level). NCERT 6th-10th science is sufficient.

syllabus.subject: General Awareness & Current Affairs
syllabus.topics: Current affairs, Indian history, geography, polity, economics, sports, awards, important days, railway GK

study_plan.tasks: RRB Group D asks 25 science questions (10th level). Read NCERT Science 6th-10th. Physics: motion, force, electricity. Chemistry: acids-bases, metals, carbon compounds. Biology: life processes, reproduction, heredity. Start Lucent GK.
Basic maths from R.S. Aggarwal — percentage, ratio, time & work (10th level). Reasoning: series, coding, analogy. Solve Kiran's previous papers daily.
Daily mock tests. Physical Efficiency Test preparation — running, weight lifting. Current affairs of last 3 months. Target: 70+ out of 100 in CBT.
